content: Treating ICM's intrinsic novelty reward as an implicit energy-based temperature term for Soft Q-Learning creates an adaptive maximum entropy reinforcement learning framework that automatically scales exploration with an agent's current uncertainty about its environment. Instead of manually tuning Soft Q-Learning's entropy temperature hyperparameter to control exploration, the ICM's measured novelty of observed states directly adjusts the temperature: higher novelty (more uncertainty) raises temperature to encourage broader sampling, while lower novelty (well-understood states) lowers temperature to greedily exploit known optimal actions.
